displayName.text: Haidilao Hot Pot Markham - 海底捞火锅

text.text:
Haidilao is like the most fun hot pot spot.

Its not ayce. And average 60-80 per person depending on how much you order. BUT learn a secret code or two to get free items during your visit. You can also learn some DIY soup vase from the sauce bar. Very interactive experience, not just eating.

The lines are not as long anymore, but there could still be so wait during rush hours. Service is not as good as before as well. Had to ask for water and soup refill a couple times.

text.text:
Love this place! The quality of the food is absolutely on point. I got the pork bone broth and my boyfriend got the tomato broth — both were delicious. It’s definitely a bit of a splurge, but honestly worth it, especially to try at least once.

Our usual spot is Liuyishou for all-you-can-eat, but the service at Haidilao was next level — super attentive and thoughtful. Huge thank you to Daniel for taking such great care of us! All the staff were amazing, and a special shoutout to the hand-pulled noodle guy — such a fun experience!

The drinks were so yummy too. The only thing missing was watercress 🥹 — my favorite veggie for hot pot — so that was a little bummer. Otherwise, everything was perfect!

text.text:
My colleague highly recommended Daimaru Shabu Shabu, and now I can see why! We first tried to visit last Sunday, but the wait times were crazy — 3 hours in Scarborough and 2.5 hours in Markham — so we decided to come back another day. This time, the Markham location initially had a 1.5-hour wait, but we got lucky and were seated within 10 minutes!

This is the only all-you-can-eat Japanese hot pot restaurant I know of, and that probably explains why it’s always so busy. It was our first hot pot experience, and the staff made it super easy and enjoyable. Big shoutout to Annie and the entire team — they were quick, efficient, and very friendly throughout.

We tried almost everything on the menu, going with Tom Yum and Spicy soup bases. The soups were okay, but the meats and veggies were fresh and of good quality. The overall experience was great, and even with the 2-hour dining limit, we never felt rushed.

Definitely worth the visit — we’ll be coming back again!

text.text:
I had a very disappointing experience at this McDonald’s due to the behavior of the manager, Sofie. I came in the morning to order food and also wanted to purchase holiday gift cards, a total of $50 broken into $10 cards. When Sofie got involved, her approach was immediately aggressive and dismissive, not just toward me but also toward her own staff who were trying to help.

She stated they could not sell the gift cards because it would “hold up the line” and take too long. This made no sense, as there was literally no one behind me in the drive thru. On top of that, I was still asked to park and wait for my food anyway, so the claim about delaying service was clearly not valid.

I fully understand if a location is extremely busy and cannot accommodate extra requests, but that was not the case here. The real issue was the attitude. As a manager, you should lead by example and treat both customers and employees with respect. The way this situation was handled felt unnecessary, unprofessional, and inconvenient.

This experience reflects very poor customer service, and Sofie would benefit from additional training in management, communication, and customer relations.

text.text:
I had a very frustrating experience at this McDonald’s location.

I placed a mobile order through the official McDonald’s app and went to the counter to pick it up. Despite clearly showing staff my order number, confirmation, and detailed receipt, I was repeatedly told that my order did not exist. The staff were unhelpful and dismissive, and I was left waiting at the counter.

After a long delay, a manager came over, but the attitude remained unprofessional. It took about 20 minutes for them to finally locate my order. By the time I received my food, everything was completely cold.

The explanation given was internal confusion caused by multiple ordering systems. This is unacceptable. I used McDonald’s official app—internal system or management failures should never be the customer’s problem.

What made the experience even worse was that no apology was offered at any point, even after the mistake was acknowledged. I felt disrespected throughout the interaction, and the way I was treated raised serious concerns about fairness and professionalism.

This location clearly has serious issues with staff training and management. Very disappointing experience.

text.text:
We had previously been to Joey's in Ottawa and wanted to come to one in the Toronto area.
We ordered the Hummous again, and it was just as great.  Fresh fried Pitas just adds that nice touch.  We also had the Happy Hour Joey's Cheesburger and the Crispy Tempura Prawns.  The Burger was tasty, juicy and just the right size for an Appy version.  The Crispy Tempura Prawns were also nice.  But the Duo of Donuts was the perfect end (if you like a dessert which is just a touch tart).  We also had a treat of the Creme Brulee Cheesecake which was even better than the Duo ... and I really like Lemon Curd.  Wow.... wow .... wow ...

Our server, AJ was super friendly and guided us well.  AJ was attentive and made our time memorable.

Because we came a bit earlier, 5:00 pm ish the noise level was actually perfect for us.  It was lively enough but we could still talk at a 'normal' volume.

We will definitely be coming back to Joey's at Markville.   Food, Service and Atmosphere were all great!

Thank you AJ.

My absolute favorites were the grilled squid, shrimp and lamb skewer— they were outstanding. The food quality is consistently very high, and the service is great as well. It’s always busy and clearly very popular for a reason. Highly recommend this place!

displayName.text: Chiang Rai Thai Kitchen and Bar

text.text:
In my opinion the best thai food I have experienced so far and I love thai food. Every dish that we tried here was exceptional and full of flavours. We were greeted by a sweet and friendly waitress who service was exceptional (I wish I asked her name). The food was made with every detail in mind - the presentation and not just the taste.

I recommend:

For appetizers - spring rolls and coconut soup.
(The creamiest coconut soup I have ever tried and I enjoyed the texture. It was served on hot plate and it was perfect for a cold day)

Main course - I highly recommend islamic noodles and spicy fried chicken.

If you appreciate sweet fruity flavours like me - pineapple fried rice served inside a carved pineapple is a must try.

I normally am not a fan of basil rice due to its mild flavours but this place has a IT flavour for basil rice too.

Overall a great place to go again. We were very full but next time we will try their desserts as well.

displayName.text: Casa Victoria Fine Dining and Banquet

text.text:
My family celebrated a birthday and we arranged a banquet meal with lots of add-ins like whole roast suckling pig and additional lobster. This is an exquisite Chinese restaurant that is truly authentic avoiding the hybrid modern/mix cuisine you might see at other restaurants. You will taste the true quality and flavour of ingredients the way they prepare each dish. It is this type of execution that makes you so proud of Cantonese cuisine for its sophistication.

The restaurant decor is characterized in the art-deco decor with elaborate finishings. Most importantly, my family found the service to be warm and professional. We dined in a private room with a dedicated server - Hua was her name who was very kind and attentive. Constantly ensuring our variety of teas were hot and full. She went out of her way to be get us to take home desserts that we liked. I would say service was outstanding and unparalleled for a Chinese restaurant. We recommend it and will return.

You got to order the French Toast if you have a breakfast or brunch sweet tooth, the dish wont disappoint!

The food was well presented, larger portions and tasted unique and delicious. Prices can be on the higher side but the quality of the food and servicing sizes makes up for that.

Service was attentive but I always worry a little when the gratuity is automatically added to the bill for larger parties. I get that it's more work and definately harder to service but it's also slippery slope to servers ignoring the table (which I should make clear never happened at the restaurant). It's a point of caution and something to be aware of.

Though I server did forget a few things we did ask for but very quickly brought them to the table when reminded. I suspect they had a few more tables to attend to that morning than expected.

Overall solid brunch experience and recommend you try this dinning spot that's at little off the beaten path in Markham, Unionville. Happy Brunching!

The restaurant is quite big and can fit a lot of people. A lot of families there. Their service was speedy. The food came out pretty fast.

Everything is decently affordable. I love that all of their meals come with a complimentary drink (coffee, tea, milk tea, etc). We got 3 dishes to share (with 4 drinks) and it only came out to $15/person after tax and tips!

Out of the 3 dishes, the only one that amazed me was this dish called, baked bolognese spaghetti and tomato chicken steak on rice. The half rice and half spaghetti combo worked really well and it was delicious.

I'd definitely go back and try other dishes!

displayName.text: Gyuyaki 焱 Markham - Japanese BBQ & Sushi

text.text:
This restaurant is basically an all you can eat Korean BBQ with some sushi options. If you've never been, they give you raw meat and you cook it yourself. The food is good but because the restaurant is always busy, the staff will kick you out early.

They have a pretty good variety of side dishes also and as I mentioned, you can also order some sushi rolls but not sashimi (raw fish). They also have a soup, salad, dessert and ice cream bar. Lots of choices!

The restaurant has around 120 seating capacity which is not really that big. Because of that, the place fills up really fast. They're always busy and there's always going to be a line up. You can join a virtual line online which may save you a bit of time but we still ended up waiting for another 10 minutes before getting our table.

Price is about $50 per person for a 2 hour time limit which is the standard for all you can eat restaurants but because the restaurant is packed, not only is it very noisy but they'll really push the 2 hour seating time on you.

The menu says the last call to order food is at your last 20 minutes. The hostess also mentioned this policy while we were being seated. Again, pretty standard for all you can eat restaurants.

However, our server gave us the last call an hour and a half in (10 minutes early), then proceeded to ask us to leave 5 minutes before our 2 hour mark. Not cool! The service itself was pretty average, nothing special. The most memorable moment was being kicked out early.

The restaurant is located in a plaza with free parking but surprisingly it's always packed. It's very difficult to get a parking spot and there are no other parking lots close by either.

Being kicked out of a noisy restaurant and going through the trouble of finding parking might be a deal breaker but at least the food makes up for it. Up to you if it's worth the trouble or not. Maybe the Mississauga location is less busy.

text.text:
The restaurant named "Little Ring Café" in Markham, co-owned by Halina Tam, a former Miss Hong Kong, and her husband. It has quickly gained popularity for serving authentic Hong Kong-style comfort food with attentive and friendly service, where the owner is notably polite and caring about the dining experience.

We tried the dishes include deep-fried fish rice with sauce and 2 noodle sets with pork chop--both praised for good taste though portions may be slightly smaller than similar eateries.

The food is authentic and flavorsome, reflecting classic Hong Kong cuisine.

Service is attentive and warm, with the owner actively engaging to ensure guests feel welcome.

Seating may involve a wait due to the restaurant's popularity but the friendly environment is encouraging.

The café aims to provide comfort and a heartful dining experience, making it a place where guests can feel the owner's passion.

It offers a genuine taste of Hong Kong with a personal touch from its celebrity owner, making it an appealing spot for fans of authentic HK food and friendly service. Check the lemon tea out if you havn't try yet.

The restaurant named "Little Ring Café" in Markham, co-owned by Halina Tam, a former Miss Hong Kong, and her husband. It has quickly gained popularity for serving authentic Hong Kong-style comfort food with attentive and friendly service, where the owner is notably polite and caring about the dining experience.

We tried the dishes include deep-fried fish rice with sauce and 2 noodle sets with pork chop--both praised for good taste though portions may be slightly smaller than similar eateries.

The food is authentic and flavorsome, reflecting classic Hong Kong cuisine.

Service is attentive and warm, with the owner actively engaging to ensure guests feel welcome.

Seating may involve a wait due to the restaurant's popularity but the friendly environment is encouraging.

The café aims to provide comfort and a heartful dining experience, making it a place where guests can feel the owner's passion.

It offers a genuine taste of Hong Kong with a personal touch from its celebrity owner, making it an appealing spot for fans of authentic HK food and friendly service. Check the lemon tea out if you havn't try yet.

text.text:
Took the parents out to Little Ring Café and honestly… not bad! Prices leaned a bit high, but the portions were massive.

Overall? A solid 7.1/10. Would go back to explore more of the menu 👀

friends really recommended this place so i decided to try it out. we ordered a few dishes but i found all the pastas to be overly salty and the pasta was not fully cooked for my liking (chefs probably consider that al dente)
salsiccia (pizza) - my friend really like this pizza that consist of sausage and bell pepper, but it personally was not for me at all. dough was too wet and mushy, it just tasted like eating ground meat with peppers, 4/10

papardelle funghi - decent dish, i really like all the mushroom flavour it’s quite strong but also very salty. i did not like the thick and long pasta shape, it was just very hard to share, 6.5/10
rigatoni vodka - probably my favourite of the night, i like the ratio of sauce, bacon and pasta. pasta was good, not too sour but also no wow factor
overall, personally i believe there are better places for similar foods, for cheaper. paid $170 for 4 dishes which is kinda crazy.  going to this place for date night maybe is nice but personally not it for me. servers had good service and was attentive

text.text:
We celebrated a family birthday at Il Postino and had an absolutely wonderful experience from start to finish. Making the reservation online was very easy and convenient, and their team replied to our email inquiry promptly and professionally, which made planning smooth and stress-free.

We had an excellent dining experience, and the cozy private room arranged for us contributed greatly to the comfort and enjoyment of our celebration. The atmosphere was warm, intimate, and charming, perfect for a special occasion without feeling overly formal.

The service was truly exceptional. The young lady (Melanie Sala) serving us tonight was the best of the best , warm, attentive, patient, and genuinely thoughtful. She offered great recommendations, checked in at the perfect moments, and made the entire experience feel personal and welcoming. We felt incredibly well taken care of throughout the evening.

The food was beautifully presented and full of rich, authentic Italian flavours. The price was very reasonable for the quality and portion we received, truly great value for the dishes served. The chef did a bravo job, and it was clear that every plate was prepared with care, skill, and passion.

Thank you to the Il Postino team for making our birthday celebration memorable and meaningful. We highly recommend this restaurant for anyone seeking delicious Italian cuisine, warm hospitality, and a wonderful overall experience.

text.text:
Perfect spot for a date night right on Main Street Unionville! We went during the summer and the patio was such a nice place to enjoy dinner. We started with the Caesar salad and the Polpette (meatballs), and boy, were those meatballs good. They were well-seasoned, had the perfect firm-yet-soft texture, and the sauce was incredibly flavorful. We used the fresh, fluffy bread to dip into the tomato sauce, which was a delight.

For mains, I highly recommend the mushroom and truffle pizza; it was super fragrant and cooked to perfection. We also tried their special at the time, the chicken pesto rigatoni. I was worried the chicken might be dry, but it was cooked just right, and the sauce was creamy without being too heavy. My only small complaint is that I would have loved a bit more protein in the pasta, but the flavor was spot on. Ended with the tiramisu which hit the spot. Definitely coming back!

First tried their Bulgogi Eggs Benedict on uber eats. It was 10/10 delicious so I decided to dine in on the next go.

Had the Char Siu Eggs Benny this time around and a Coco Pandan latte.

The Char Siu was also 10/10. What gives their breakfast a 10 for me on both dishes is that their sides aren’t just an afterthought. I can appreciate a well seasonsed side salad and spicy home fries.

Can here around 11 on a Friday and the place was fairly busy, but service was still very quick and friendly. It’s definitely a “trendier” spot in the area, definitely an atmosphere you’d expect downtown which I though was a nice change in the Markham area.

Lots of parking as well, would highly recommend as a good spot to grab brunch in the ~downtown Markham area.

text.text:
Was looking for a brunch spot in Markham and told to try out Sisters and Co based on instagram and google reviews.
Location is basically in the middle of no where in an industrial park area but was suprised to see a line up of people waiting to go in. Location is small and tables are packed but it’s a sign of a busy place.
Young staff who are super friendly and fast!
We ordered breakfast Benny’s with an Asian twist. One was a char sui pork Benny and another was a bulgogi. Wow never had that before an was amazed. I’ll admit it was on a slightly sweeter side so not the same as eating salty bacon but totally delish and with hot sauce was super yummy. Also ordered a french toast which was double wow! Massive toasted with a cream on top excellent presentation too big to eat the whole thing. Best way to come here is in a group of 2-4 people max and share! I ordered a London fog and was so floral and flavourful and a matcha strawberry drink also great. Overall very happy. There is a time limit to eat but we came closer to lunch and crowds died down so they didn’t rush me out as I thought they would. Menu has great lunch options too. Happy to try this place and will definitely come back!

text.text:
I've loved coming to Sisters & Co for brunch and I've gone a couple times already! I do recommend coming here if you're looking for a fusion brunch spot but if you're looking to have a long yap session, this isn't the place as there's a time limit of 1.5 hrs. There's usually a wait especially on the weekends (about 30-45 min) and unfortunately, you cannot get on the waitlist online so you have to reserve a spot in person.
As you can see, I quite enjoy their spaghetti bolognese and all the other items that I've tried like the cereal milk french toast, black sesame pancakes, chicken and waffles, and bulgogi benedict have all been good! Overall, a good brunch spot to check out :)

text.text: It's a very aesthetic hotpot place with a variety of different foods. It was AYCE and soup based are extra on top. For the money spent, it's a bit much as there are other places that have AYCE for a cheaper price. Spent around $70 after tax and tip ($38 around for AYCE and an extra $10-20 for soup base, plus tax and tip). There were a lot of seafood options, fried foods, vegetables and even desserts. I loved the green tea ice cream and the fruits especially. Had a lot of photo taking opportunities as it reminded me of ancient Chinese buildings. If you're willing to spend this amount for the experience I'd say go ahead, but if you're not, there's definitely cheaper AYCE hotpot places somewhere else.

text.text:
Absolutely disgusting and disgraceful. Came in around 5:30 AM and the place looked like a trash dump. Garbage bins overflowing from the night before, trash everywhere, and a Big Mac box just sitting there. This restaurant was clearly not cleaned at all.
Let’s be real — if the dining area is this filthy, the kitchen is probably even worse. That’s beyond unacceptable for a food business and honestly a health risk.
This is 100% on lazy, incompetent management. I tried to speak to the manager and she ignored me, then had the nerve to whisper “it’s none of your business.” Excuse me? Cleanliness in a restaurant is EXACTLY the customer’s business.
Dirty restaurant, rude manager, zero professionalism, zero standards. This place is being run like a joke. McDonald’s should be ashamed of whoever is in charge here

text.text:
Ordered some breakfast to the hospital after a long night at the birthing ward.  Asked the new mother what she wanted and she has mentioned hotcake so okay alright we place the order.
While placing the order I noticed that hotcakes on their own was there but seemingly greyed out, I searched for it and it gave me the option to add to order so I did so...while trying to checkout it said the item is no longer available...seemingly like they ran out or something. Weird cause it still allows any hotcake meal to be ordered, strange they didn't run out when it means more $$ being brought in as it's tied to extra items not really wanted.

Delivery shows up and it was great, rest of the order was great...but we noticed the hotcakes don't live up to McDonald's own freshness/food safety standards.  As you can see in the picture, McDonald's stock shows the hotcakes were prepped Nov 27 and to enjoy by Nov 29.  Likely took the hotcakes out the freezer on the 27 and packaged and refrigerated which has it's rightful shelf life before it's supposed to be thrown out.  My order was after that date.

"Great job delivering a kid, here are some stale pancakes that don't live up to McDonald's Canada's own standards and hopefully you don't get sick."

text.text:
Pretty good location, just got majorly renovated. The staff are friendly but sometimes they miss things which given that it's pretty busy is to be expected. Overall very happy with this place as a McD's.

Update: Feb 2022 - the staff are pretty efficient for pick up at the front counter even with a long drive thru line. Very accurate with my order including condiments.

Update: June 2022 - They've removed the barrier at the pick up counter but the ones at the cash register area is still there.

Update: Aug 2022 For some reason the app is acting up so a couple of times it's not worked to do the mobile order and I've had to go to the cashier or the drive thru to get the order done.  :(

Update Jan 2025: Staff at this location are pretty friendly and helpful. They still have table service when you order in app.

Update Aug 2025: Restaurant has remained clean and tidy even though it gets busy and the tables fill up on a weekend morning. Staff are polite and friendly when they bring your order for table service.

Update Nov 2025: there's a mobile order shelf by the pick up counter. It has the mobile order number labeled for people to pick up their order.

text.text:
Feels transported to Korea!
It was a fun event for all of us as a family.
My wife and I ordered the vegan BBQ option ($21pp), and the kids ordered pork belly, Kalbi and steak.  We had fun and food and as delicious. The staff is amazing and constantly circling to help or clean up.
It does take a long time to roast the veggies but it allows all of us to enjoy our time together.
You can ask for extra lettuce to wrap your food (awesome!) and the condiments of picked cucumbers and seaweeds were so tasty! (A bit salty as expected)
Will come back soon!
